get.e: I will get a new book from the library.

good.d: Good means something is nice, helpful, or works well.
good.e: The weather is good for playing outside.

go.d: to move or travel to a place
go.e: I go to the library every week.

going.d: To move or travel to a place
going.e: I'm going to the park to play with my friends.

got.e: I got a new bike for my birthday!

great.e: I had a great time at the party last night.

give.e: I will give my friend a piece of my cookie.

game.e: My friends and I like to play board games together.

government.d: the group that runs and makes laws for a country
government.e: The government of our country is in the capital city.

god.d: The most important being in some religions
god.e: My family prays to God every night.

group.d: A number of people or things together
group.e: Our class is a group of 25 students.

general.d: relating to the main or common parts
general.e: In general, I like all kinds of fruits.

getting.e: I'm getting a new backpack for school.

given.d: something that is provided or allowed
given.e: The teacher gave us a homework assignment.

games.e: My favorite games are puzzles and board games.

guys.d: a group of people, usually male
guys.e: The guys on my baseball team are my best friends.

girl.e: My sister is a very kind and smart girl.

guy.e: My friend is a nice guy who likes to play soccer.

gonna.d: Going to, something you plan to do
gonna.e: I'm gonna go to the park later today.

gave.e: The teacher gave each student a new pencil.

gets.e: The student gets good grades in school.

goes.d: To move or travel to a place
goes.e: The bus goes to the park every day.

groups.d: A number of people or things together
groups.e: The class was divided into small groups.

gone.d: When something or someone is not here anymore.
gone.e: The cookie was gone because the dog ate it.

ground.d: The surface of the earth that we walk on.
ground.e: The squirrel buried the nut in the ground.

guess.d: To try to figure out the answer to something you're not sure about.
guess.e: I will guess that the dog's name is Buddy.

gold.d: A very expensive and shiny yellow metal
gold.e: My medal is made of solid gold.

girls.e: The girls played on the swings at the park.

giving.d: The act of providing something to someone else
giving.e: Giving my little sister a hug always makes her happy.

green.d: The color of grass and leaves

goal.d: Something you want to achieve or accomplish.
goal.e: My goal is to learn to ride a bike this summer.

growth.d: When something gets bigger or stronger over time
growth.e: The plant showed a lot of growth over the summer.

german.e: My grandparents speak the German language.

gives.d: To provide or supply something to someone
gives.e: My grandma gives me a hug every time I visit.

generally.d: Usually or most of the time
generally.e: I generally like to play outside in the afternoon.

george.d: A common name for a boy
george.e: My friend George likes to play outside.

greater.d: Greater means bigger or more important than others.
greater.e: The lion is greater in size than the tiger.

global.e: The global problem of hunger affects many countries.

gas.d: The fuel that makes cars and trucks go
gas.e: We need to buy gas for the car.

goals.d: Things you want to do or achieve
goals.e: My goals for this year are to read more books and learn a new hobby.

germany.e: My family visited Germany and saw the beautiful castles.

gotta.d: a shorter way to say 'have got to' or 'need to'
gotta.e: I gotta finish my homework before I can play.

gun.e: Only police officers and soldiers should use guns.

growing.d: getting bigger or taller over time
growing.e: The little plant is growing taller every day.

grand.e: The grand castle on the hill looked amazing.

glad.e: I was glad to see my friend after a long time.

grow.d: To become bigger or taller over time
grow.e: The flower will grow very tall in the garden.

gay.e: The clown had a gay and funny personality.

glass.d: A clear, hard material that windows and cups are made of
glass.e: She put her drink in a glass on the table.

generation.d: A group of people born around the same time
generation.e: Each generation passes down traditions to the next.

greatest.d: The best, most important, or most powerful.
greatest.e: My grandma is the greatest baker I know.

gain.d: To get or receive more of something
gain.e: The puppy gained weight as it grew.

governor.d: A leader who manages a state or region.
governor.e: The governor opened a new park in our town.

gift.e: I got a new toy as a gift.

guard.d: A person who watches over and protects others
guard.e: The security guard watches the building.

grade.d: a level or step, like in school
grade.e: I'm in the fourth grade and learning a lot this year.

goods.d: Things that are made or grown and can be bought and sold.
goods.e: The store sells many different goods, like clothing and toys.

grant.d: To allow or agree to give someone something they want.
grant.e: The teacher will grant us extra time to finish the project.

grew.d: To become bigger or taller over time.
grew.e: The little seedling grew into a tall tree.

girlfriend.d: a girl who is a close friend
girlfriend.e: She said her best friend is her girlfriend.

granted.d: To allow or give permission for something.
granted.e: The teacher granted the student's request for more time.

guns.d: Weapons that shoot bullets or other projectiles.
guns.e: The police officer had a gun to protect people.

grown.d: To become bigger or taller over time
grown.e: The little plant has grown into a big tree.

gender.d: the state of being male or female
gender.e: Some animals have different genders, like male and female.

golden.d: a bright, shiny yellow color like the sun
golden.e: The sunset painted the sky in a beautiful golden glow.

guilty.d: When you have done something wrong
guilty.e: The boy felt guilty after he broke his sister's toy

giant.e: The giant tree reached up to the sky.

grace.d: Moving in a smooth, elegant way
grace.e: The dancer moved with grace across the stage.

greek.d: Related to the country of Greece
greek.e: Greek food like olives and feta cheese is delicious.

governments.d: The people and systems that run a country or state.
governments.e: The government makes laws to keep us safe.

gained.e: The runner gained a lead over the other runners.

golf.d: a game played on a big grassy area with a club and ball
golf.e: John loves playing golf with his dad on the weekends.

guest.d: a person who visits your home
guest.e: The birthday party had lots of fun guests.

grounds.d: The land or outdoor area around a building or place
grounds.e: The children played on the school grounds during recess.

gate.d: A door or opening in a fence or wall
gate.e: The park has a big gate at the entrance.

gross.d: something very big or a lot of something
gross.e: There were gross amounts of dirt on the floor.
